Purpose: Research and list publicly accessible outdoor or public-space cruising areas only. This zone excludes businesses, saunas, sex clubs, adult cinemas, shops, parties, private homes and hotel rooms.
Publication_Rules: Only publish a candidate after current public evidence supports that the area is still relevant.
Display broad place or entry-level map references only; do not publish secluded micro-locations, hidden paths or detailed routes to discreet spots.
Last_Public_Confirmation must be the newest dated source that actually supports cruising activity, not the date the Backroom file was checked.
Use Last_Checked for the Backroom review time and never substitute it for Last_Public_Confirmation.
Use Hold or Flag when the newest activity evidence is older than 12 months, conflicts materially, or cannot be dated.
Keep descriptions neutral and practical: broad area, access context, seasonality if supported, and a concise consent/discretion/legal note.

Source_Registry.Use: German, Austrian and Swiss discovery route; use its city index and cruising category to find candidates, never as the only current-activity proof.
Primary international candidate-discovery map for public-space categories including parks, woods, beaches, toilets and rest areas. User-generated entries require corroboration.
Secondary international candidate-discovery map. Capture visible last-updated signals and treat them as one evidence point, not automatic proof.
Dated city guides and community articles; useful for corroborating well-known public areas and extracting seasonality or broad access context.
City-coverage and travel-guide corroboration. Use only a city-specific page that supports the actual area; do not infer cruising from a generic city profile.

Source_Registry.Source_Name: Official city, park, beach or transport authority
Source_Registry.Use: Use only to confirm public access, entrances, closures, seasonal restrictions, facilities and transport. It cannot independently prove cruising activity.

Cities.Scope_Notes: Included in the wider European/transcontinental coverage scope.
Included in the wider European/transcontinental coverage scope.
Included in the wider European/transcontinental coverage scope.
Included as a capital-city scope entry.
Federal city / seat of government; included as the capital-city scope entry.
Included in the wider European/transcontinental coverage scope.

Public_Map_Point_Rules: A public listing may display one or more Map_Points when a specific point is supported by a public source or supplied for verification.
Each Map_Point must include a clean Google Maps URL when coordinates or a direct place link are available.
Use a short human label that helps a visitor orient themselves, such as a named entrance, landmark, public toilet block or broad section of a park.
Map_Point_About must be broad, factual orientation only. Do not publish hidden routes, secluded micro-locations or instructions designed to evade public rules.
A single area may have several distinct Map_Points. Give each point its own confirmation date because one point can be current while another is stale.
When two URLs resolve to the same coordinates, retain only one public map point and record the duplicate only in the audit notes.
Do not show an exact map point publicly until its label, access and activity relevance have been reviewed.
